registry.description: Brokenness is the state of being shattered — the Hebrew root carries images of fracture, crushing, and collapse. It is used of physical breaking but equally of the inner collapse of the person under grief, judgment, or the weight of their own sin. Brokenness is not entirely negative in Scripture: the broken and contrite heart is what God does not despise (Psalm 51). There is a brokenness that is the end of self-sufficiency and the beginning of dependence on God — which makes it a threshold rather than merely a wound.

terms.medium_def:
1) to break, break in pieces
1a) (Qal)
1a1) break, break in or down, rend violently, wreck, crush, quench
1a2) to break, rupture (fig)
1b) (Niphal)
1b1) to be broken, be maimed, be crippled, be wrecked
1b2) to be broken, be crushed (fig)
1c) (Piel) to shatter, break
1d) (Hiphil) to cause to break out, bring to the birth
1e) (Hophal) to be broken, be shattered
Aramaic equivalent: te.var (תְּבַר "to break" H8406)
